title: Inline validation: invalid email format prevents successful registration
description: Verifies the registration form rejects an invalid email format and provides visible feedback.

steps.description: Type "Bad Email User" into the name field

title: Inline validation: missing required name shows error
description: Verifies the registration form requires a name and shows a visible error when omitted.

steps.description: Verify text "Name is required" is visible

title: Inline validation: missing password shows error
description: Verifies the registration form requires a password and shows a visible error when omitted.

steps.description: Type "No Password User" into the name field
Verify text "Password is required" is visible

title: Inline validation: role not selected shows error
description: Verifies the registration form requires a role selection and does not submit without it.

steps.description: Type "No Role User" into the name field
Verify text "Role is required" is visible

title: Filter submissions by UNGRADED status, integrity score range, and student name search
description: Verifies the teacher can combine status toggle, score range, and search to narrow the submissions list.

steps.description: Click "Show Filters" to expand the filter panel
Fill "0" into the minimum integrity score field
Fill "100" into the maximum integrity score field

title: Switch between ALL, GRADED, and UNGRADED toggles
description: Verifies the dashboard updates the visible state when the teacher switches submission status filters.

steps.description: Click "Show Filters" to expand the filter panel

title: Filter submissions using a narrow integrity score range
description: Verifies the teacher can input a tight integrity score range and see the page reflect the applied filter state.

steps.description: Click "Show Filters" to expand the filter panel
Fill "70" into the minimum integrity score field
Fill "85" into the maximum integrity score field

title: Search submissions by student name or assignment title
description: Verifies the search input can be used to filter the submissions list.

steps.description: Click "Show Filters" to expand the filter panel

title: Enter non-numeric integrity score input and apply filters
description: Verifies the filter panel handles non-numeric integrity score input with a visible validation error or safe empty result state.

steps.description: Click "Show Filters" to expand the filter panel
Fill "abc" into the minimum integrity score field

title: Handle min integrity score greater than max integrity score
description: Verifies the UI prevents or clearly indicates an invalid score range when minimum exceeds maximum.

steps.description: Click "Show Filters" to expand the filter panel
Fill "90" into the minimum integrity score field
Fill "10" into the maximum integrity score field

title: Integrity score range rejects out-of-bounds values (below 0 or above 100)
description: Verifies the integrity score inputs handle values outside the expected 0–100 range with a visible error or safe behavior.

steps.description: Click "Show Filters" to expand the filter panel
Fill "-1" into the minimum integrity score field
Fill "101" into the maximum integrity score field

title: Empty results state when filters yield no matches
description: Verifies the dashboard shows a clear empty state when applied filters produce no submissions.

steps.description: Click "Show Filters" to expand the filter panel

title: Bulk grade selected submissions with score and feedback
description: Verifies a teacher can select submissions and apply the same score and feedback to all selected items successfully.

steps.description: Click checkbox on an individual submission

title: Complete bulk grade modal submission shows success confirmation
description: Verifies the bulk grade modal accepts a valid score and optional feedback and shows a visible success confirmation after grading.

title: Bulk grade modal requires score (validation error)
description: Verifies leaving the score empty triggers a visible validation error when attempting to grade selected submissions.

steps.description: Click checkbox on an individual submission
Click the "Grade" button in the bulk grade modal

title: Bulk grade button reflects selection count
description: Verifies the Bulk Grade button indicates the number of selected submissions (N) after selecting multiple items.

steps.description: Click checkbox on an individual submission
Click checkbox on a second individual submission

title: Select All selects visible submissions and enables bulk grading
description: Verifies using Select All selects visible submissions and the Bulk Grade action becomes available.

title: Bulk grade modal can be dismissed without grading
description: Verifies the teacher can open the bulk grade modal and close it without applying grades.

steps.description: Click checkbox on an individual submission

title: Bulk grade with score only (no feedback) succeeds
description: Verifies submitting a valid score while leaving feedback empty can still successfully bulk grade selected submissions.

steps.description: Type "85" into the score field in the bulk grade modal

title: Attempting bulk grade with no selection is prevented
description: Verifies the teacher cannot start a bulk grade action when no submissions are selected.

title: Save a rubric-based grade successfully with auto-calculated total and feedback
description: Verifies a teacher can switch to Rubric Mode, enter valid rubric scores, see the total update, add feedback, and submit a saved grade confirmation.

steps.description: Click on a submission to open the detail panel
Click "Overall Mode" to switch to "Rubric Mode"
Type "20" into the Content score field
Type "18" into the Grammar score field
Type "19" into the Structure score field
Type "17" into the Originality score field
Type "Good structure overall. Please proofread for grammar and tighten the thesis." into the feedback field

title: Reject out-of-range rubric score with a visible validation error
description: Verifies entering an out-of-range rubric score (e.g., 30) prevents submission and shows a score range validation message.

steps.description: Click on a submission to open the detail panel
Click "Overall Mode" to switch to "Rubric Mode"
Type "30" into the Content score field
Verify text "must be within" is visible

title: Auto-calculated total updates when a single criterion score changes
description: Verifies the displayed total changes after editing one rubric criterion score in Rubric Mode.

steps.description: Click on a submission to open the detail panel
Click "Overall Mode" to switch to "Rubric Mode"
Type "10" into the Content score field
Type "10" into the Grammar score field
Type "10" into the Structure score field
Type "10" into the Originality score field
Type "15" into the Content score field

title: Allow boundary values 0 and 25 for rubric criteria
description: Verifies rubric criteria accept minimum and maximum allowed values and still allow grade submission.

steps.description: Click on a submission to open the detail panel
Click "Overall Mode" to switch to "Rubric Mode"
Type "0" into the Content score field
Type "25" into the Grammar score field
Type "0" into the Structure score field
Type "25" into the Originality score field
Type "Boundary values test." into the feedback field

title: Prevent submitting when a required rubric score is missing
description: Verifies the UI blocks submission and shows a visible error when at least one rubric criterion score is left empty.

steps.description: Click on a submission to open the detail panel
Click "Overall Mode" to switch to "Rubric Mode"
Type "12" into the Content score field
Type "12" into the Grammar score field
Type "12" into the Structure score field

title: Reject negative rubric score with validation messaging
description: Verifies entering a negative value (e.g., -1) for a rubric criterion is rejected and shows a visible range validation message.

steps.description: Click on a submission to open the detail panel
Click "Overall Mode" to switch to "Rubric Mode"
Type "-1" into the Grammar score field

title: Allow submitting with feedback left blank (if optional)
description: Verifies that a grade can be submitted with valid rubric scores even when no feedback is entered, or else shows a clear feedback-required validation if feedback is mandatory.

steps.description: Click on a submission to open the detail panel
Click "Overall Mode" to switch to "Rubric Mode"
Type "15" into the Content score field
Type "15" into the Grammar score field
Type "15" into the Structure score field
Type "15" into the Originality score field

title: Switching to Rubric Mode reveals the four rubric criteria fields
description: Verifies that after switching modes, the rubric criteria (Content, Grammar, Structure, Originality) are visibly present for scoring.

steps.description: Click on a submission to open the detail panel
Click "Overall Mode" to switch to "Rubric Mode"

title: Word count progress bar updates after typing
description: Verifies the word count progress bar is visible and reflects typed content in the editor.

steps.description: Verify element "Word count progress bar" is visible

title: Due date countdown is visible on the editor
description: Verifies students can see the due date countdown/timer UI on the editor page.

steps.description: Verify element "Due date countdown" is visible

title: Escape toggles focus mode and returns to normal mode
description: Verifies the Escape key toggles focus mode, changing visible UI state (e.g., focus mode indicator or hidden side panels) and can be toggled back.

steps.description: Verify element "Word count progress bar" is visible

title: Open a draft from Drafts and save changes with keyboard shortcut
description: Verifies a student can open an existing draft from the Drafts list, edit it in the editor, and save using Ctrl+S with a visible success confirmation.

steps.description: Click "Drafts" in the primary navigation menu

title: Drafts list is visible and shows at least one draft card/row when drafts exist
description: Verifies the Drafts page renders a visible list area for drafts and allows selection of a draft when drafts are present.

steps.description: Click "Drafts" in the primary navigation menu

title: Open a draft into the editor from the Drafts page
description: Verifies clicking a draft from the Drafts list opens the editor view for that draft.

steps.description: Click "Drafts" in the primary navigation menu
Click on the first visible draft in the drafts list

title: Externally removed draft shows Draft not found error when clicked
description: Verifies that selecting a draft that no longer exists results in a visible "Draft not found" error message.

steps.description: Click "Drafts" in the primary navigation menu
Click on the draft item labeled "Removed" or "Not found" if present
Verify text "Draft not found" is visible

title: Draft not found error state provides a visible way to return to drafts list
description: Verifies the error state for a missing draft offers a visible navigation option to go back to Drafts (or similar) and returns the user to the list.

steps.description: Click "Drafts" in the primary navigation menu
Click on the draft item labeled "Removed" or "Not found" if present
Verify text "Draft not found" is visible
Click the "Back to drafts" (or "Return") button/link if visible

title: View profile information on Profile page
description: Verifies a logged-in user can open the Profile page and see their profile fields displayed.

steps.description: Click on "Profile" in the main navigation

title: Edit full name and class and save successfully
description: Verifies a logged-in user can edit full name and class fields and see a success confirmation after saving.

steps.description: Click on "Profile" in the main navigation
Type "Test Student Updated" into the "Full name" field

title: Create a new assignment and verify it appears in the assignments list
description: Verifies a teacher can create an assignment with required fields and see it listed afterward.

steps.description: Type "E2E Assignment - Short Essay" into the Title field
Type "A short essay about academic integrity." into the Description field
Type "500" into the Max word count field
Type "2099-12-31" into the Due date field
Verify text "E2E Assignment - Short Essay" is visible

title: Validate due date cannot be in the past when saving an assignment
description: Verifies the UI blocks saving when the due date is earlier than today and shows a visible validation message.

steps.description: Type "E2E Assignment - Past Due" into the Title field
Type "2020-01-01" into the Due date field
Verify text "due date must be in the future" is visible

title: Required field validation: cannot save assignment with empty title
description: Verifies the UI shows a visible error and prevents saving when the title is missing.

steps.description: Type "2099-11-30" into the Due date field
Type "750" into the Max word count field
Verify text "Title is required" is visible

title: Required field validation: cannot save assignment with empty due date
description: Verifies the UI shows a visible error and prevents saving when the due date is missing.

steps.description: Type "E2E Assignment - Missing Date" into the Title field
Type "400" into the Max word count field
Verify text "Due date is required" is visible

title: Word limit validation: prevent saving when max word count is zero
description: Verifies the UI rejects a non-positive word limit and shows a visible validation message.

steps.description: Type "E2E Assignment - Zero Limit" into the Title field
Type "2099-10-10" into the Due date field
Type "0" into the Max word count field
Verify text "Max word count must be greater than 0" is visible

title: Cancel out of assignment creation without saving
description: Verifies a teacher can exit the create assignment screen and the new assignment is not added to the list.

steps.description: Type "E2E Assignment - Do Not Save" into the Title field
Verify text "E2E Assignment - Do Not Save" is not visible

title: Create a new class and see it listed
description: Verifies a teacher can create a class with section details and the new class appears in the class list.

steps.description: Click on "Classes" in the main navigation
Type "Biology 101 - Section A" into the class name field
Verify text "Biology 101 - Section A" is visible

title: Cancel out of the Create Class dialog without creating a class
description: Verifies a teacher can dismiss class creation and no new class is added from the canceled attempt.

steps.description: Click on "Classes" in the main navigation
Type "Chemistry 201 - Section B" into the class name field
Verify text "Create Class" is not visible

title: Create class validation: required fields block submission
description: Verifies required fields are enforced when creating a class and an error is shown if missing.

steps.description: Click on "Classes" in the main navigation

title: Create class with long name is handled gracefully
description: Verifies a very long class name can be submitted or produces a clear error without breaking the page.

steps.description: Click on "Classes" in the main navigation
Type "Advanced Interdisciplinary Studies in Biological Systems and Computational Modeling - Spring Term Section A" into the class name field
Verify text "Advanced Interdisciplinary Studies" is visible

title: Open Similarity page and view flagged pairs list
description: Verifies a logged-in teacher can access Similarity and see a list of flagged pairs.

steps.description: Click on "Similarity" in the main navigation

title: Expand a flagged pair and view side-by-side comparison
description: Verifies expanding a flagged pair reveals a side-by-side text comparison for both submissions.

steps.description: Click on "Similarity" in the main navigation
Click on the first flagged pair in the list

title: Expanded comparison shows text from both submissions
description: Verifies the expanded comparison area contains visible text content for both the left and right submissions.

steps.description: Click on "Similarity" in the main navigation
Click on the first flagged pair in the list

title: Collapse an expanded pair to return to the list view
description: Verifies a teacher can collapse the expanded comparison and return to the compact pair row view.

steps.description: Click on "Similarity" in the main navigation
Click on the first flagged pair in the list
Click on the same flagged pair again to collapse

title: Filter flagged pairs by student name
description: Verifies filtering narrows the flagged pairs list based on a student name query.

steps.description: Click on "Similarity" in the main navigation

title: Filter with no matches shows an empty state
description: Verifies using a filter query that matches no records shows an appropriate empty state message.

steps.description: Click on "Similarity" in the main navigation

title: View Teacher Analytics charts on initial load
description: Verifies that a logged-in teacher can open Analytics and see the integrity score distribution and submission trends charts.

steps.description: Click on "Analytics" in the main navigation

title: Integrity score distribution chart is visible
description: Verifies that the integrity score distribution visualization is present on the Analytics page.

steps.description: Click on "Analytics" in the main navigation

title: Apply date range filter updates analytics view
description: Verifies that changing the date range filter updates the analytics charts content shown on screen.

steps.description: Click on "Analytics" in the main navigation
Click on the date range filter control

title: Change date range selection to a preset option
description: Verifies that selecting a preset date range (for example, last 7 days) updates what is displayed for analytics.

steps.description: Click on "Analytics" in the main navigation
Select "Last 7 days" from the date range dropdown

title: Clear or reset date range filter returns to default view
description: Verifies that resetting the date range filter returns the analytics page to its default state.

steps.description: Click on "Analytics" in the main navigation
Click on the date range filter control

title: Open an assignment from Student Dashboard and land in Editor
description: Verifies a student can open an assignment card from the dashboard and is taken to the editor screen.

steps.description: Click on the first visible assignment card

title: Student Dashboard shows notifications section on load
description: Verifies the dashboard displays a notifications area or header indicating where notifications appear.

title: Student Dashboard shows assignment cards section on load
description: Verifies the dashboard displays an assignments area indicating where assignment cards are listed.

title: Empty-state messaging appears when no assignments are available
description: Verifies the dashboard provides a clear instructional message when the student has no enrolled classes/assignments.

title: Empty-state does not allow opening an assignment when none are listed
description: Verifies that when the dashboard is in an empty state, the student cannot navigate to the editor via an assignment card.

steps.description: Scroll to the assignment cards area
Verify element "assignment card" is not visible

title: Dashboard to Drafts quick link navigation (if present)
description: Verifies the student can use a quick link from the dashboard to reach their drafts list (when the link is available).

title: Dashboard to Submissions quick link navigation (if present)
description: Verifies the student can use a quick link from the dashboard to reach their submissions list (when the link is available).

title: Dashboard to Profile quick link navigation (if present)
description: Verifies the student can navigate from the dashboard to their profile via a visible link or menu item.

title: Student can open Student Analytics and see performance and integrity summaries
description: Verifies a logged-in student can access Student Analytics and sees graphs/metrics content.

steps.description: Click on "Student Analytics" in the main navigation

title: Student Analytics page shows graphs and metric sections when data exists
description: Verifies the analytics page renders visible graph/metric sections rather than an empty state when data is available.

steps.description: Click on "Student Analytics" in the main navigation

title: Student Analytics page supports viewing multiple metric areas by scrolling
description: Verifies the student can scroll the analytics page and additional metric content remains visible/loads properly.

steps.description: Click on "Student Analytics" in the main navigation
Scroll to the bottom of the page

title: Student with no submission history sees empty-state message on Student Analytics
description: Verifies the Student Analytics page shows the correct empty-state message when there is no analytics data.

steps.description: Click on "Student Analytics" in the main navigation
Verify text "No analytics data available" is visible

title: Student Analytics empty-state does not show graph sections
description: Verifies that when the empty-state message is shown, graph/metric sections are not shown in a misleading way.

steps.description: Click on "Student Analytics" in the main navigation
Verify text "No analytics data available" is visible

title: Student can navigate away from Student Analytics to another student page
description: Verifies the Student Analytics page does not trap navigation and the student can move to another student route via UI.

steps.description: Click on "Student Analytics" in the main navigation
Click on "Submissions" in the main navigation

title: DOCX export option is visible and selectable in the editor export menu
description: Verifies the editor export menu opens and shows a DOCX option that can be selected.

steps.description: Click on "Editor" in the main navigation

title: Student exports an essay to PDF from the editor
description: Verifies a logged-in user can initiate a PDF export from the editor export menu.

steps.description: Click on "Editor" in the main navigation

title: Teacher can open Export Grades flow from the dashboard
description: Verifies a logged-in teacher can access the dashboard and open the export grades control.

steps.description: Click on "Dashboard" in the main navigation

title: Teacher selects CSV in Export Grades and sees an on-screen response state
description: Verifies selecting CSV triggers an export attempt and the UI provides a visible response (success or error state).

steps.description: Click on "Dashboard" in the main navigation

title: Export controls are present on both Editor and Dashboard pages for eligible users
description: Verifies export entry points are visible in the editor for essays and on the teacher dashboard for grades.

steps.description: Click on "Editor" in the main navigation
Click on "Dashboard" in the main navigation
